title: AH_P_REACTOR_HIGH
text: High reactor pressure alarm. Setpoint at 2900 kPa. Operator should verify compressor recycle valve position, inspect condenser duty, and reduce feed if pressure trend remains rising for 30 seconds.

title: AH_T_REACTOR_HIGH
text: High reactor temperature alarm. Trigger at 155 C. Expected operator response is to increase cooling medium flow, verify exotherm control loop, and prepare controlled feed cutback.

title: TE Fault 03: Feed Temperature Step
text: A step increase in feed temperature causes elevated reactor temperature and pressure, followed by condenser load increase. Alarm signature commonly starts with AH_T_REACTOR_HIGH then AH_P_REACTOR_HIGH.

title: TE Fault 09: Condenser Cooling Water Loss
text: Condenser cooling performance degrades. Pressure rises rapidly and compressor alarms may cascade. Root cause is reduced heat removal in condenser section.

title: TE Fault 15: Valve Stiction
text: Control valve stiction creates oscillatory behavior and repeated alarm bursts. Signature includes alternating flow and pressure alarms with periodicity tied to loop integral action.

title: Cause Effect for F09
text: Primary path: cooling water loss -> condenser temperature increase -> reactor pressure increase -> compressor discharge pressure alarm. Secondary interaction may trigger reactor temperature alarms due to reduced condensation.

title: Synthetic Incident: Night Shift Pressure Flood
text: During nominal operation, condenser cooling failed at t=0. Within 20 seconds AH_COND_TEMP_HIGH and AH_P_REACTOR_HIGH activated. Operator first acknowledged alarms, then reduced feed by 15 percent and opened recycle. Flood intensity dropped by t=90 seconds.

title: PtX Offshore Compressor Disturbance Response
text: For offshore PtX operation, compressor instability under rapid load swings requires staged response: isolate non-critical loads, maintain hydrogen loop pressure margin, and prioritize alarms linked to containment and overpressure.
